title: Ach Batch Window Orchestration
description: A payments-and-settlement workflow that controls release, routing, and execution timing across multiple rails and liquidity constraints. Focus case: ach batch window orchestration.
assumptions: Batches are canonical units: orchestration proceeds at batch granularity rather than per-payment.
Settlement windows and rails are deterministic and known ahead of scheduling (discrete windows).
Funding_account balances and reserve tokens are authoritative and consumed/released atomically by orchestration steps.
Clearing_operator availability is required for operator-validation steps; operator responses are eventual but within operational SLAs.
risks: Liquidity shortfalls: reserved funds may be insufficient at execution time causing failed settlement.
Misrouting: incorrect rail or channel binding may route a batch to an ineligible destination causing delay or returns.
Authorization failures: missing or revoked credentials may block release of a batch.
Operator downtime or latency: external operator outage can stall validation and prevent batches from meeting window deadlines.
Race conditions between reserve/release actions leading to double-commit or orphaned holds.
